Lake Hume Village, NSW · Est. 1936

Where the Lake
Meets the Good Life

Wake to shimmering water views, explore endless wilderness, and discover a region bursting with world-class dining, culture, and adventure — just 15 minutes from Albury.

Why Lake Hume Resort

Your lakeside escape awaits

Perched on the shores of one of Australia's most spectacular waterways, Lake Hume Resort is more than a place to stay — it's a gateway to an extraordinary region. Just 15 minutes from Albury and 10 minutes from the airport, yet a world away from the everyday.



Choose from 26 motel rooms and spacious 2 and 3-bedroom lakeside cottages, each designed for comfort with breathtaking views of the lake, gardens, and surrounding mountains.



Check Availability
🌊

Lakeside Location

Directly on the shores of Lake Hume with views across shimmering water to the mountains beyond.

✈️

Easy to Reach

10 minutes from Albury Airport with daily direct flights from Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane.

👨‍👩‍👧‍👦

Family Friendly

Pool, mini-golf, tennis, beach access and endless outdoor adventures for all ages.

🍽️

World-Class Dining

On-site restaurant plus the Albury-Wodonga region's acclaimed dining scene just minutes away.

Activities & Experiences

Adventure on every horizon

From the tranquil to the exhilarating, Lake Hume offers experiences that stay with you long after you leave.

Trail Riding

Cycling the Rail Trail

The High Country Rail Trail hugs the lake's shoreline — breathtaking views and sealed paths for all skill levels.

On the Water

Kayaking & Paddleboarding

Explore tranquil coves and drowned river valleys at your own pace. Equipment hire available on-site.

Murray River

World-Class Fishing

Cast for Golden Perch, Brown Trout and the iconic Murray Cod in one of Australia's premier fishing destinations.

View All Activities
Water Sports

The lake is yours to conquer

Lake Hume is a haven for water skiing, wakeboarding, jet skiing, and sailing. The resort's proximity to boat ramps makes launching effortless — and with sunsets like these, you'll never want to leave.


Lake Hume holds six times the volume of Sydney Harbour.



Plan Your Visit
Natural Attractions

A region of remarkable beauty

Within an hour of the resort, discover dramatic landscapes, ancient gorges, sweeping lookouts, and the shimmering heart of the Murray River system.

National Park

Woomargama National Park

The jewel of the Greater Hume region. 4WD trails, hiking, and Hanel's Lookout for panoramic sunset views across the valley.
Bushwalking

Table Top Mountain

A striking geological landmark visible from the lake. Guided bushwalking and spectacular photography opportunities await.
Riverside Walks

Gateway Island Parklands

10km of pristine Murray River frontage, towering red gums, and walking tracks along the mighty river between Albury and Wodonga.
45 min Drive

Woolshed Falls & Beechworth Gorge

Cascading falls and ancient granite gorges steeped in gold-rush history — stunning, easy-to-access natural beauty in Victoria.
Explore All Attractions
Food & Wine

A culinary region that punches above its weight

Albury-Wodonga's dining scene rivals Australia's major cities — with multiple Chef's Hat restaurants, vibrant laneway bars, celebrated Italian, Asian fusion, and a craft beer movement that's firmly taken hold.


On-site, the resort's restaurant and bar offers relaxed dining with lake views. And just 15 minutes away, a world of culinary adventure awaits.

Fine Dining

Chef's Hat–awarded restaurants serving modern European cuisine from premium regional produce.

Café Culture

Riverside brunches, locally roasted coffee, and stylish eateries with Murray River views.

Global Flavours

Authentic Italian pasta, Asian fusion, Mexican street food — all within minutes of the resort.

Craft Beer & Bars

Small-batch breweries, laneway bars, and natural wine — a slice of Melbourne in regional NSW.

Chef's Hat Restaurants
Chef's Hat Award
Miss Amelie
Wodonga · Modern European

Set in the historic former Wodonga railway station, this award-winning restaurant is the crown jewel of the region's fine dining scene — elegant, intimate, and unforgettable.

Visit Website
Chef's Hat Award
Bistro Selle
Albury · European Bistro

An intimate European-style bistro celebrated for flawless technique, innovative dishes, and a wine list that perfectly complements every course.

Visit Website
Chef's Hat Award
Yardbird
Albury · Spanish / French / Italian

Drawing on Spanish, French and Italian influences, Yardbird delivers a refined dining experience in an effortlessly relaxed setting with a stunning courtyard.

Visit Website
Chef's Hat Award
Level One Wine Bar & Restaurant
Albury · Modern Australian

A local favourite for modern Australian dishes, natural wines, and polished, unfussy service. The wine list alone is worth the visit.

Visit Website
More Great Venues Nearby
The River Deck
Albury · Modern Australian

Nestled in Noreuil Park on the Murray River banks. The go-to for long lunches with spectacular views.
Canvas Eatery
Albury · Café & Tapas

Behind MAMA museum. Stylish brunches by day, tapas and cocktails on the terrace by night.
Norma Wine and Pasta
Albury · Italian

A charming corner restaurant with a massive following for freshly made authentic pasta.
Din Dins
Albury · Asian Fusion

Vibrant flavours from Japan, Korea, Vietnam, and Thailand all under one roof.
Temperance & General
Albury · Laneway Bar

Industrial-style laneway bar — a slice of Melbourne's bar culture in regional NSW.
Beechworth Bakery
Albury · Bakery

An iconic regional institution. Mandatory stop for pies, beestings, and nostalgic pastries.
View Full Dining Guide
Getting Here

The perfect midway point

Lake Hume Resort sits naturally between Australia's major eastern cities — an easy drive or short flight from Sydney, Melbourne, Canberra, and Brisbane.
